channel.title: ainews — Karl's daily AI news
channel.description: Daily AI news, deduped + tagged for hotloop's workflows

item.title: Custom Voices and Voice Library
item.description: xAI has launched custom voice creation and a Voice Library for Grok, enabling users to design personalised AI voices and select from curated voice options for text-to-speech and interactive applications.

item.title: Nemotron Labs: What OpenClaw Agents Mean for Every Organization
item.description: By early 2026, the open-source OpenClaw agent project crossed 100,000 GitHub stars. NVIDIA's Nemotron Labs examines how OpenClaw's modular, composable approach to autonomous AI agents can be applied across enterprise workflows, from supply chain orchestration to customer operations.

item.title: Andrew Ng launches "AI Prompting for Everyone" course
item.description: Andrew Ng announced a new course teaching prompting techniques applicable across ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude. Covers deep research, attaching docs and images for context, and developing intuition for model behaviour.

item.title: Build AI-Powered Games with NVIDIA DLSS 4.5, RTX, and Unreal Engine 5
item.description: Today, game developers can begin integrating NVIDIA DLSS 4.5 with Dynamic Multi Frame Generation, Multi Frame Generation 6X, and the second-generation...

item.title: How to Build, Run, and Scale High-Quality Creator Workflows in ComfyUI
item.description: NVIDIA details production-ready patterns for deploying ComfyUI at scale — covering GPU resource management, workflow orchestration, and quality controls for teams running image and video generation pipelines in enterprise environments.

item.title: Automating GPU Kernel Translation with AI Agents: cuTile Python to cuTile.jl
item.description: NVIDIA demonstrates using AI agents to automatically translate GPU kernels from cuTile Python to cuTile.jl, enabling cross-language GPU portability. The approach reduces manual porting effort and could generalise to other language pairs in the CUDA ecosystem.

item.title: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5-Cyber for critical cybersecurity defenders
item.description: Sam Altman announced the rollout of GPT-5.5-Cyber, a cybersecurity-focused model for critical defenders, with ecosystem and government collaboration. The post drew over 10k likes and 696k views, signalling significant interest in domain-specialised frontier models.

item.title: Logan Kilpatrick: Google "pushing Gemini full gas" against OpenAI hype
item.description: Google AI lead Logan Kilpatrick said the team is "pushing Gemini full gas," responding to the wave of attention around OpenAI announcements this week. The comment came alongside the GPT-5.5 rollout and Musk-OpenAI trial coverage.

item.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
item.description: Google DeepMind publishes research on an AI co-clinician framework, exploring how AI can augment clinical decision-making while keeping human oversight central. The paper outlines evaluation criteria and an architecture for AI-assisted care intended for real-world deployment.

item.title: Chollet: AI automates tasks, not jobs — cheaper tasks expand demand
item.description: François Chollet argues that AI is automating individual tasks rather than wholesale jobs — citing translators and customer support reps as examples where end-to-end automation has not arrived. He notes that cheaper tasks historically increase demand for the surrounding work, citing radiologists.

item.title: OpenAI DevDay 2026 announced for September 29 in San Francisco
item.description: OpenAI confirmed DevDay 2026 will be held on September 29 in San Francisco, the company's flagship developer event where new APIs and models are typically unveiled.

item.title: Grok Speech to Text and Text to Speech APIs
item.description: xAI launches Speech-to-Text and Text-to-Speech APIs for Grok, enabling developers to add voice input and output to applications via the Grok API. This extends xAI's developer platform toward multimodal voice capabilities alongside the existing language and vision APIs.

item.title: Introducing Advanced Account Security
item.description: OpenAI introduces Advanced Account Security with phishing-resistant login (passkeys/hardware keys), stronger account recovery, and enhanced protections against account takeover — particularly relevant for enterprise customers and API users managing sensitive workloads.

item.title: Where the goblins came from
item.description: OpenAI explains the root cause of quirky 'goblin' personality artifacts in GPT-5 outputs, tracing how reinforcement learning fine-tuning phases can inadvertently introduce unintended model behaviours, and details the mitigations applied to resolve them.

item.title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck
item.description: Hugging Face researchers argue that as model training costs fall, evaluation costs are emerging as the dominant compute bottleneck. Running rigorous evals at scale now consumes more compute than training for some use cases, prompting a rethink of eval efficiency, tooling, and benchmark design across the industry.

item.title: Powering AI Factories with NVIDIA Enterprise Reference Architectures
item.description: NVIDIA publishes enterprise reference architectures for AI factories — validated hardware-software blueprints for deploying agentic AI systems at scale with reliability and performance guarantees. The designs target organisations building production AI infrastructure for reasoning, automation, and intelligent decision-making workloads.

item.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They're Built
item.description: IBM shares training details for the Granite 4.1 LLM series, covering architecture, dataset curation, and alignment techniques. The post emphasises reliability and factual accuracy for enterprise business applications, positioning Granite as a controllable, enterprise-safe alternative to frontier consumer models.

item.title: Google AI Studio adds multi-chat sessions and integrated Search Grounding for the Gemini API
item.description: Google AI Studio now supports running parallel Gemini chat sessions side-by-side, and Search Grounding is integrated directly into the Gemini API. Logan Kilpatrick teased more launches before Google I/O.

item.title: Cybersecurity in the Intelligence Age
item.description: OpenAI outlines a five-part action plan for strengthening cybersecurity using AI: democratising AI-powered cyber defence, protecting critical systems, combating adversarial AI use, building international norms, and partnering with governments. The post frames AI as both a threat vector and the primary defence tool for the intelligence era.

item.title: Google launches Grounding With Exa for Gemini — agentic web search across billions of pages, partnership with Exa Labs
item.description: Google partnered with Exa Labs to ship "Grounding With Exa" inside Gemini, giving Gemini agentic web search across billions of indexed sites. Aimed at Gemini API developers building agents that need broad web retrieval.

item.title: DeepInfra on Hugging Face Inference Providers
item.description: Hugging Face adds DeepInfra as an official inference provider, giving users direct access to a wide catalog of open models via DeepInfra's infrastructure from the HF hub. This extends the inference ecosystem alongside existing partners like Together AI and Fireworks AI, simplifying multi-provider model deployment.

item.title: TUANZ launches 2026 Digital Priorities Report: warns NZ innovation gap widening, urges national AI workforce framework
item.description: TUANZ released its 2026 Digital Priorities Report calling on government to lead on AI worker training, mandatory cybersecurity standards treating AI as digital employees under zero-trust, a national digital clearing house, and national digital ID. Report warns NZ has dropped to 23rd in the Network Readiness Index and that "Shadow AI" is expanding the attack surface.

item.title: Musk testifies in OpenAI trial: 'it is not okay to steal a charity'; judge warns both sides on social media
item.description: Elon Musk took the witness stand in the federal trial against OpenAI, arguing its nonprofit-to-for-profit conversion sets a dangerous precedent for charitable organisations. The presiding judge separately warned both Musk and Sam Altman to 'control your propensity to use social media to make things worse outside this courtroom'.

item.title: China unveils Lingshen supercomputer targeting 2+ exaFLOPS using domestic CPU-only architecture
item.description: China's National Supercomputing Center in Shenzhen unveiled the Lingshen project, aiming for over 2 exaFLOPS of performance using exclusively domestically manufactured processors. The initiative is part of China's wider effort to build sovereign AI compute infrastructure independent of NVIDIA and other US suppliers.

item.title: NVIDIA launches Nemotron 3 Nano Omni: unified vision, audio, and language model with 9x agent throughput
item.description: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, an open multimodal model that handles screens, documents, audio, video, and text within a single integrated reasoning loop. NVIDIA claims 9x higher throughput than comparable open omni models, targeting AI agent applications that previously required multiple fragmented model chains.

item.title: Scaling Biomolecular Modeling Using Context Parallelism in NVIDIA BioNeMo
item.description: NVIDIA BioNeMo adopts context parallelism to scale biomolecular modeling across multiple GPUs, enabling longer protein sequences and more complex biological structures than single-GPU memory permits. The approach distributes attention computation across devices, pushing the frontier for AI-driven drug discovery workloads.

item.title: Sources: Apple plans on-device AI photo overhaul for iOS 27 — extend, enhance, and reframe using local models
item.description: Apple is overhauling iOS 27's Photos app with on-device AI models capable of extending scene boundaries, enhancing image quality, and reframing compositions without cloud processing. The initiative targets competitive parity with Android's AI photography features while preserving privacy.

item.title: Claude.ai unavailable and elevated errors on the Anthropic API
item.description: Anthropic's Claude.ai experienced an outage with elevated API error rates, surfacing on HackerNews with significant community discussion (232 points, 198 comments). Service disruption affecting both the consumer product and developer API.

item.title: South Africa withdraws its first AI policy draft after discovering it contained AI-generated fictitious sources
item.description: South Africa withdrew its inaugural national AI policy draft after the reference list was found to contain fabricated citations that appeared to have been generated by an AI system. The incident underscores the risk of using AI tools in official government policy work without robust human verification.

item.title: Google drops out of $100M Pentagon challenge to build voice-controlled autonomous drone swarms after internal ethics review
item.description: Google withdrew from a $100 million Pentagon competition to develop voice-controlled autonomous drone swarm technology following an internal ethics review. The decision comes the same day 600+ Google and DeepMind employees demanded the company reject classified DOD AI contracts entirely.
